articles.headline: Harry Kane vs. Erling Haaland: How do England, Norway stars compare? Key stats before World Cup clash
articles.description: Who will come out on top in the World Cup quarterfinal, Harry Kane or Erling Haaland? Here is what the stats say.

images.name: Harry Kane Erling Haaland [1296x729]
images.alt: An image of Harry Kane alongside one of Erling Haaland.
images.credit: Photo by Luis Cano/Jam Media/Getty Images and Photo by Richard Sellers/Sportsphoto/Allstar via Getty Images

images.name: Erling Haaland [1296x729]
images.alt: Erling Haaland wears a viking helmet.
images.credit: (Photo by Julian Finney - FIFA/FIFA via Getty Images)

images.name: Harry Kane penalty [1296x729]
images.alt: Harry Kane takes the penalty against Mexico.

images.name: Odegaard: Norway's quarterfinal against England a 'massive test'
images.caption: Odegaard: Norway's quarterfinal against England a 'massive test'

articles.headline: Police officer injured, arrests made amid London disorder after France beat Morocco
articles.description: A police officer was injured and four people were arrested after disorder broke out in London following France's 2-0 win against Morocco in the World Cup quarterfinal on Thursday.

articles.headline: Instead of its biggest World Cup star, Pulisic was U.S.'s biggest mystery
articles.description: In his prime at a World Cup on home soil, this summer was Christian Pulisic's moment, but for a number of reasons, he failed to meet it.

articles.headline: World Cup quarterfinals: How we got here, predictions, odds
articles.description: Four weeks after the World Cup began, the quarterfinals are underway, with each team three wins from lifting the trophy. Next up, Spain vs. Belgium.

competitions.altGameNote: FIFA World Cup, FIFA World Cup

article.headline: Netherlands thump Sweden 5-1 as Cody Gakpo, Brian Brobbey bag World Cup doubles
article.description: Brian Brobbey and Cody Gakpo scored two each as Netherlands cruised to a 5-1 World Cup victory over Sweden to go top of Group F.
article.linkText: Netherlands thump Sweden 5-1 in statement win

images.name: Potter: Sweden loss to Netherlands an 'opportunity to grow'
images.caption: Potter: Sweden loss to Netherlands an 'opportunity to grow'

article.story:
Brian Brobbey and Cody Gakpo scored two each as Netherlands cruised to a 5-1 World Cup victory over Sweden to go top of Group F.

Oranje boss Ronald Koeman sprang something of a surprise by selecting Brobbey ahead of Crysencio Summerville, a goal scorer in their opening 2-2 draw with Japan, but the decision was quickly vindicated as the Sunderland striker scored twice in the opening 17 minutes.

After Sweden had a goal ruled out at the end of the first half, Gakpo hit two in the first 10 minutes of the second half, and although Anthony Elanga pulled one back for Graham Potter's Sweden just before the hour, substitute Summerville had the final say in the 90th minute.

Only five minutes in, Brobbey laid the ball off to Tijjani Reijnders, who swept it left for Gakpo to send in a low cross for Brobbey to poke in, and the second goal was almost a mirror image as Denzel Dumfries crossed low from the right and Brobbey again prodded home.

Sweden, who came to life after the first hydration break, thought they had a goal back late in the half, but Gustaf Lagerbielke was one of several Sweden players offside as he headed in Benjamin Nygren's free kick.

Instead, Gakpo was able to kill off Sweden early in the second half. When Summerville, on for Donyell Malen at the break, sent in a low cross that Brobbey could not quite stretch far enough to get his hat trick, but Gakpo was left with an easy finish at the back post.

Eight minutes later, Netherlands punished a mistake from Alexander Isak with a rapid counter as Summerville laid the ball off for Gakpo to cut in from the left and fire home.

Just before the hour Elanga gave Sweden something to cheer as he ran on to a through ball from Isak and clipped it beyond Bart Verbruggen, but Summerville sparked fresh Dutch celebrations in the 90th minute as he swept home from the edge of the box.

keyEvents.text: First Half begins.
Goal! Netherlands 1, Sweden 0. Brian Brobbey (Netherlands) right footed shot from very close range to the centre of the goal. Assisted by Cody Gakpo with a cross.
Goal! Netherlands 2, Sweden 0. Brian Brobbey (Netherlands) right footed shot from very close range to the bottom left corner. Assisted by Denzel Dumfries with a cross.
Delay in match for a drinks break.
Delay over. They are ready to continue.
Substitution, Netherlands. Crysencio Summerville replaces Donyell Malen.
Second Half begins Netherlands 2, Sweden 0.
Goal! Netherlands 3, Sweden 0. Cody Gakpo (Netherlands) left footed shot from very close range to the centre of the goal. Assisted by Denzel Dumfries with a cross.
Gabriel Gudmundsson (Sweden) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Goal! Netherlands 4, Sweden 0. Cody Gakpo (Netherlands) right footed shot from the left side of the box to the bottom left corner. Assisted by Crysencio Summerville following a fast break.
Substitution, Sweden. Anthony Elanga replaces Alexander Bernhardsson.
Substitution, Sweden. Besfort Zeneli replaces Jesper Karlström.
Substitution, Sweden. Lucas Bergvall replaces Benjamin Nygren.
Goal! Netherlands 4, Sweden 1. Anthony Elanga (Sweden) left footed shot from the centre of the box to the bottom left corner. Assisted by Alexander Isak with a through ball following a fast break.
Substitution, Netherlands. Teun Koopmeiners replaces Frenkie de Jong.
Substitution, Netherlands. Guus Til replaces Tijjani Reijnders.
Delay in match for a drinks break.
Delay over. They are ready to continue.
Substitution, Netherlands. Memphis Depay replaces Brian Brobbey.
Yasin Ayari (Sweden) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Substitution, Sweden. Taha Ali replaces Yasin Ayari.
Lucas Bergvall (Sweden) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Goal! Netherlands 5, Sweden 1. Crysencio Summerville (Netherlands) right footed shot from outside the box to the bottom left corner. Assisted by Memphis Depay.
Substitution, Netherlands. Noa Lang replaces Cody Gakpo.
Substitution, Sweden. Elliot Stroud replaces Gabriel Gudmundsson.
Delay in match because of an injury Crysencio Summerville (Netherlands).
Delay over. They are ready to continue.
Second Half ends, Netherlands 5, Sweden 1.

stats.name: aerialsWon
stats.description: Total number of duels where the ball is in the air and possession is won

stats.name: duelsLost
stats.description: Total number of duels over the possession of the ball where a player loses the ball - doesn't include 'overrun' situations where the attacking player takes on an opponent but the ball runs away from them out of play or to an opponent.

stats.name: duelsWon
stats.description: Total number of duels for possession of the ball where possession was won (aerial won + ground duels won)

stats.name: groundDuelsWon
stats.description: Total number of duels where possession is won and ball is on the ground; including total contests, total tackles, fouls, being fouled, dispossessions and lost challenges.

stats.name: duels
stats.description: Total number of direct player-on-player confrontations for possession of the ball (aerial + ground duels)

stats.name: groundDuels
stats.description: Total number of duels over possession where ball is on the ground; including total contests, total tackles, fouls, being fouled, dispossessions and lost challenges - doesn't include 'overrun' situations where the attacking player takes on an opponent but the ball runs away from them out of play or to an opponent.

stats.name: duelWinPct
stats.description: The percentage of duels won.

stats.name: turnover
stats.description: An unsuccessful touch. A player only touched the ball and lost possession – bad control

stats.name: wonContest
stats.description: Total dribbles where a team player beats an opponent – no over-runs or defensive 1v1s

stats.name: totalContest
stats.description: A dribble past a player is attempted (contests always involve two players) – it does not include 'overrun' situations (where the attacking player takes on an opponent, but the ball runs away from them and then out of play or to an opponent) or 'defensive 1v1' situations (where the attacking player attempts a dribble but is stopped by the defender but without a tackle occurring)
